task_completion.description: Create the required layers for the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List widget (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface) should display the specific layers: 'SHAFT', 'CENTER', 'HATCHING', 'DIMENSIONS', and 'TEXT'.
A large circle representing the shaft is visible in the main drawing canvas.

task_completion.description: Draw the rectangular keyway notch into the shaft.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular cutout is visible on the circumference of the shaft circle, breaking the continuous circular line to form a notch.

task_completion.description: Add center lines and cross-section hatching.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Horizontal and vertical lines intersect at the center of the shaft. The solid area of the shaft is filled with a diagonal hatching pattern, while the rectangular keyway notch remains empty (unhatched).

task_completion.description: Add required dimensions and text annotations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines with numbers (indicating 50, 14, and 5.5) are visible. Text labels containing 'SHAFT', 'KEYWAY', '1045', and 'DIN 6885' are placed on the drawing.
The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'shaft_keyway_section.dxf', or a save dialog is seen saving the file to the Documents/LibreCAD directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with LibreCAD's toolbars, menus, or internal command line, rather than a terminal window generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the CAD software by writing raw DXF code in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) containing raw DXF vector code.

integrity.description: The agent used the CAD hatching tool rather than manually drawing individual diagonal lines.
integrity.visual_evidence: The hatching dialog box should be visible at some point, or the hatching should appear uniformly generated in a single step rather than line-by-line.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified specifications: The shaft must have a diameter of 50mm (radius 25mm). The required layers are exactly: SHAFT, CENTER, HATCHING, DIMENSIONS, and TEXT. The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/shaft_keyway_section.dxf. Note: The keyway dimensions (14mm width x 5.5mm depth) are a custom specification and must be drawn as requested, despite not matching standard DIN 6885 dimensions. Ensure text annotations include '1045' as explicitly requested in the task description.
